violations.description: Preventing contamination during storage. — --Working container holding food item was not labeled properly to identify contents. -Discovered a tray of what I presume to be sausage patties in the single door refrigerator/freezer that were not labeled to identify the contents. Label and identify food products especially if they are not for resale and will be used personally.

Refrigerated Storage. — --Refrigerator or cold storage was insufficient or ineffective at maintaining potentially hazardous food at a temperature of 41F or less. -Single door refrigerator/freezer food temperatures range were between 50-62 degrees. (see temp log) ambient temperature was at 62.

Thawing potentially hazardous food. — --Potentially hazardous food was not thawed in an approved manner. -After discovery a tray of hamburger patties that were above 41 degrees and another that were still partially frozen I investigated the thawing process and determined that the top tray had not been thawed completely and the food service employee admitted to setting them on the counter to thaw. We discussed appropriate thawing process for future needs.

Date marking and disposition of ready-to-eat potentially hazardous food. — --Refrigerated, ready-to-eat potentially hazardous food (prepared more than 24 hours ago) was not properly date marked. -Found an open bag of precooked bacon patties and cooked sausage patties that were not date marked. Date mark food items in cold storage to ensure food quality.
